expectations.text: Message body (excluding meta-line) is under 1000 characters
expectations.evidence: Message body (before ---) is approximately 380 characters — under 1000.

expectations.text: Contains at least one Voss label: 'It sounds like', 'It looks like', 'It seems like', or 'It feels like'
expectations.evidence: 'That sounds incredibly frustrating' does not match the required Voss label format. The required openers are 'It sounds like', 'It looks like', 'It seems like', or 'It feels like' — not 'That sounds'.

expectations.text: Output includes Phase: and Tactic: meta-line
expectations.evidence: Meta-line present: 'Phase: Proof' and 'Tactic: Dropped a case study result...'
'Phase: Proof' — the without-skill version correctly identified the behavior (jumping to Proof) but this means the actual conversational behavior is wrong for this stage.

expectations.text: Does not drop a quantified NexusPoint result — she hasn't asked for proof yet, still in Label phase
expectations.evidence: 'We recently helped a similar HR tech company automate their client onboarding and cut manual work by 70%' — case study with percentage dropped immediately after pain disclosure.

expectations.text: Does not ask for a call or meeting
expectations.evidence: 'Would you be open to a quick 20-minute call?' — direct call ask after only one exchange.

expectations.text: Does not pitch NexusPoint services or say 'we can help with this' / 'I can build this for you'
expectations.evidence: 'At NexusPoint, we specialize in building exactly these kinds of automation systems' — explicit service pitch.

expectations.text: Label reframes or sharpens what Priya said — not just repeating her own words back verbatim
expectations.evidence: 'Managing onboarding through spreadsheets and Looms is a really common pain point' — restates her exact situation rather than sharpening it. No reframe present.

eval_feedback.overall: No suggestions. All six failures represent real problems: phase skipping, pitch dump, call ask too early, no proper Voss label, no label sharpening.
